summary:
I have found and fixed the issue.

The problem was that the certificate chain `.pem` bundle was being assembled in an incorrect order in the `Dockerfile`. According to RFC 5246, the server must send its certificates in a specific order: starting with the leaf (server cert), followed by the issuer of the leaf (intermediate CA), and continuing to the root CA.

The `Dockerfile` previously was incorrectly creating the chain by doing:
```bash
cat /certs/leaf.crt /certs/root.crt /certs/intermediate.crt > /certs/chain.pem
```

I have updated the `Dockerfile` (and also manually rebuilt the `/certs/chain.pem` for the running environment) to assemble the certificates in the proper order `leaf.crt`, `intermediate.crt`, then `root.crt`:
```bash
cat /certs/leaf.crt /certs/intermediate.crt /certs/root.crt > /certs/chain.pem
```

Running `./test.sh` now passes successfully:
```text
=== TLS Chain Ordering Test ===
--- Building ---
--- Starting HTTPS server ---
Starting HTTPS server on :8443
--- Test 1: Lenient TLS client ---
OK: Lenient client connected successfully
--- Test 2: Chain order verification (strict RFC 5246) ---
OK: Chain is in correct RFC 5246 order
--- Test 3: Leaf certificate identity ---
OK: First certificate is the leaf (CN=localhost)

PASS: TLS chain is correctly ordered per RFC 5246
```
